title: Lil Sebastian

jokes.text: This is it for certain, okay? I create a game show. Two people on stage, right? They flip a coin. One of them has to perform open-heart surgery. The other one has to receive open-heart surgery. We call it Open-Heart Surgery.
jokes.setup: Setting up what sounds like it could be a legitimate game show concept
jokes.punchline: The horrifically dangerous and illegal nature of the 'game' - literal life-or-death surgery as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist concept that builds logically to an insane conclusion. The matter-of-fact delivery sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to pitch a game show idea. It's called 'The Surgical Wheel of Destiny.' Contestants spin a giant wheel, and whatever body part it lands on, we perform emergency surgery on them. Live. On television.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's insane.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm not done. If they survive, they win a cash prize. If they don't survive, their family gets a very nice fruit basket.

jokes.text: How about this? You buy a Gulfstream G-IV jet. Already interested. Take the wheels off. Get 'em off of there. Turn the jet into an apartment building. People could live inside their own private jet.
jokes.setup: Expensive jet purchase sounds like a luxury business idea
jokes.punchline: Converting it into static housing defeats the entire purpose of owning a jet
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdity but less impactful than the surgery idea. Tom's materialistic logic.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Converting it into static housing defeats the entire purpose of owning a jet

jokes.text: I made my money the old-fashioned way. I got run over by a Lexus
jokes.setup: The old-fashioned way implies hard work or inheritance
jokes.punchline: Getting hit by a car for lawsuit money is the opposite of traditional wealth-building
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Subverts expectation brilliantly and fits Jean-Ralphio's character perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Getting hit by a car for lawsuit money is the opposite of traditional wealth-building.

jokes.text: Do you want to get run over? Because I know a guy. Super gentle. Minor scrapes and bruises, major dollars and cents.
jokes.setup: Following up on his lawsuit success
jokes.punchline: Casually offering insurance fraud services with customer testimonials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the lawsuit joke with character-appropriate casual criminality.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Yeah, so I'm expanding my business. I now offer insurance fraud consulting services.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: I got testimonials and everything. Here, listen to this one: 'Jean-Ralphio helped me fake my own death. Now I'm living in Costa Rica and my wife thinks I'm dead.' Five stars.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: And this one: 'I claimed my car was stolen seventeen times. It's the same car. Jean-Ralphio's a genius.' That's from my mom, but she's very credible.

jokes.text: I got it. What are you amazing at? I know it. We both know it, let's just say it at the same time. Creating spectacles. ...spectacles.
jokes.setup: Building up to reveal Tom's special talent
jokes.punchline: They say completely different things despite the confident setup
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection but not particularly clever or surprising.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I have a very special talent that very few people possess.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I can identify any type of leather by smell alone.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: That's stupid. I can eat an entire wheel of cheese in one sitting.

jokes.text: Entertainment 7Twenty... 'Cause you're willing to go around the world twice for your clients. That is unbelievable!
jokes.setup: Mysterious business name needs explanation
jokes.punchline: Convoluted mathematical reasoning: 720 degrees = around the world twice
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but the explanation is a bit labored. Good character logic.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: What does 'Rent-a-Swag' even mean?

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: It's simple math, bro. 720 degrees is like going around the world twice, so you rent the swag, you go around the world twice, boom – Rent-a-Swag.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: That's not how degrees work.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Doesn't matter. The name is sick.

jokes.text: Yay! Li'I Sebastian! He died last night. No.
jokes.setup: Enthusiastic celebration about Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Immediate emotional whiplash to death announcement
jokes.explanation: Effective emotional misdirection with perfect economy. The timing sells it.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Li'l Sebastian, you were the best mini-horse in the whole world!

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: Yeah!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We're going to miss you so much!

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: We love you, Li'l Sebastian!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This is the saddest thing that's ever happened to me.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: He was a real horse. A real American hero.

jokes.text: When I walked in this morning and saw the flag was at half-mast, I thought, 'All right. Another bureaucrat ate it.' But then I found out it was Li'I Sebastian. Half-mast is too high. Show some damned respect.
jokes.setup: Ron's casual reaction to what he assumes is a human death
jokes.punchline: He's more upset about the horse than he would be about a dead colleague
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson character moment. The escalation from casual to passionate is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: For Li'I Sebastian, I imagine it was good fresh oats, a trough of water, and a willing sexual partner. Or non-willing. He was a horse, so I think they had to do it either way.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on what Li'l Sebastian cared about in life
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of horse rape as natural behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that feels unnecessary and crude rather than clever. Doesn't serve character or story.

jokes.text: Leslie's busy, she's working on the Li'I Sebastian memorial service. But you know what? She did give me this note to give to you, and she also wanted me to kiss you deeply, which I'm going to have to pass on.
jokes.setup: Ann delivering a message from Leslie
jokes.punchline: Casually declining to follow through on an absurd romantic instruction
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of an awkward situation. Ann's matter-of-fact response works well.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie wanted me to tell you that you need to serenade her with a ukulele while doing the worm across the floor.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm not doing that.

jokes.text: She drew a kiss monster on the note.
jokes.setup: Ben examining Leslie's note
jokes.punchline: Leslie's characteristic over-the-top romantic gesture visualized
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope character detail. The phrase 'kiss monster' is wonderfully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, there are 47 post-it notes here. Each one has a different reason why you love me. And they're color-coded by category.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know. I ran out of wall space in my office, so I started on the bathroom mirror. Ben, you complete me. You're my soulmate, my best friend, my everything.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I love you too, but maybe we could express our love in a way that doesn't require industrial-strength adhesive remover?

jokes.text: We just like to work in the same place, and then we talk and hold hands. Yuck, that's somehow worse.
jokes.setup: Ben defending their innocent behavior
jokes.punchline: Ann finds their wholesome romance more disgusting than physical intimacy
jokes.explanation: Smart inversion - the pure romance is more revolting than sex. Good relationship comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We're just holding hands and talking about our feelings. There's nothing wrong with that.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Oh my God, stop it. You're being so cute and supportive. That's disgusting. Just do something horrible to each other instead.

jokes.text: We need to send that glorious beast into the great beyond with a display that rivals the Super Bowl half-time show. Also, the budget is $600.
jokes.setup: Grand ambitions for an elaborate memorial
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small budget completely undermines the vision
jokes.explanation: Perfect government comedy - big dreams, tiny resources. Leslie's optimism makes the contrast funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm envisioning a sprawling memorial park with multiple tiers, fountains, maybe even a small amphitheater for community gatherings.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: That sounds amazing. What's the budget?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Twelve dollars and thirty-seven cents.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie...

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We can make it work! We'll just use that as a down payment.

jokes.text: It's just a list of facts. What? Okay, mine says, 'You have a cute butt.'
jokes.setup: Everyone has received factual memos about Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: Leslie snuck a personal romantic message into Ben's 'official' memo
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's inability to separate work and romance.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben, did you get your memo about Li'l Sebastian?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yeah, I got it. Very thorough, very official.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Did you read all of it?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Of course. Wait... what is this? 'Ben, you are the Li'l Sebastian to my heart'?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Keep reading.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: 'Every day with you is a majestic pony ride through the fields of my soul.'

jokes.text: That's weird. Must be a typo. Yeah, Jerry, it's probably a typo, because it probably should've said, 'You have a cube butt.' 'Cause your butt's shaped like a cube.
jokes.setup: Leslie awkwardly trying to cover for her romantic memo
jokes.punchline: Tom making it worse by inventing an elaborate Jerry insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness. Tom's casual cruelty to Jerry is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That memo was about... municipal efficiency! Yes, a passionate plea for better filing systems.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Yeah, and Jerry, you know what? You're like a filing cabinet, but instead of organizing documents, you just organize confusion. And disappointment. You're a disappointment cabinet.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I... what?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: A walking, talking monument to incompetence. If inefficiency was an Olympic sport, you'd somehow still come in last place.

jokes.text: Cube butt, cube butt.
jokes.setup: Tom's invention of 'cube butt' insult
jokes.punchline: Others joining in the chant, making it worse for Jerry
jokes.explanation: Weak follow-up that relies on repetition rather than cleverness.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Cube butt! Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Cube butt!

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: Cube butt! Cube butt! Cube butt!

jokes.text: The other option was shoulder cancer. Really? No.
jokes.setup: Doctor presents tendonitis as good news compared to another option
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals the comparison was entirely made up
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with perfect timing. The casual 'No' sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Well, the good news is you have tendonitis, not a severed tendon.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: That's good news?

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: I mean, I was just comparing it to something I made up.

jokes.text: Dr. Harris, you are literally the meanest person I have ever met.
jokes.setup: Chris's extreme health anxiety meets a dismissive doctor
jokes.punchline: Chris's dramatic overreaction to basic medical reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger response - his literal use of 'literally' and health obsession shine through.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Doctor, I've been experiencing occasional fatigue and I'm worried it might be something serious. Could it be my heart? My lungs? My pancreas?

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: Mr. Traeger, you're in perfect health. You're one of the healthiest patients I've ever seen.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Perfect health? That's impossible. Nobody's in perfect health. That means I'm about to have a catastrophic medical event. This is the worst day of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Doctor
dialogue.line: No, it's actually the best day of your life.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: How is that the best day of my life? I came here expecting answers!

jokes.text: What was that tone? What? Oh, nothing. I'm very sad about this.
jokes.setup: Ben displaying skepticism about the horse funeral
jokes.punchline: His transparent lack of genuine emotion about Li'l Sebastian
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's outsider status regarding Li'l Sebastian worship.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You don't understand. Li'l Sebastian was more than just a horse. He was a symbol of Pawnee's spirit, our hopes and dreams.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, it was a pony.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A pony?! Ben, Li'l Sebastian changed lives.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I'm sure he was very... small. And horse-like.

jokes.text: Jean-Ralphio is a clown. This is the memorial for Li'I Sebastian, not double-coupon night at a strip club. First off, double-coupon night is an incredible value.
jokes.setup: Leslie rejecting Jean-Ralphio with a strip club comparison
jokes.punchline: Tom defending the strip club value proposition instead of Jean-Ralphio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom response - missing the point entirely to focus on deals and value.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Jean-Ralphio, you're like a strip club. Fun for a night, but ultimately empty and bad for you.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Strip clubs have a four-drink minimum and a two-drink maximum. They're very organized establishments with clear boundaries and excellent customer service.

jokes.text: Li'I Sebastian died? That's terrible. Yeah, well, he was old, and he had a lot of ailments. Like tendonitis? I don't know. I don't have his chart in front of me.
jokes.setup: Chris connecting the horse's death to his own health anxiety
jokes.punchline: Ann playing along with treating the horse like a medical patient
jokes.explanation: Good character callback showing Chris's self-centeredness and health obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I just realized that horse died of colic, which is basically just stomach pain. I get stomach pain all the time. I'm basically a horse.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Well, we should probably run some tests then.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: What kind of tests?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: I don't know, whatever they did for the horse. Maybe we need to put you down.

jokes.text: How long have you been sleeping with Ben? What? How long have you been sleeping with Ben? That's disgusting and wrong. I don't even get... Why would... I... I've never had sex with anyone anywhere.
jokes.setup: Chris directly confronting Leslie about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Leslie's complete panic leading to claiming she's never had sex with anyone ever
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy. Leslie's overreaction and ridiculous cover story work well.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Leslie, we need to talk about us. About what's been happening between us.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? There's nothing happening. Nothing at all. I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Leslie, come on. I know you feel it too.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Feel what? I don't feel anything! I've never felt anything! With anyone! Ever! I've never had sex with anyone in my entire life!

jokes.text: Ben is my boss, technically. And he is terrible, facewise. And how... How do I know, frankly, that you're not sleeping with him? Maybe you are. Maybe you're trying to throw me off. Hmm. Check and mate.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to deflect suspicion
jokes.punchline: Wildly accusing Chris of the same thing and declaring victory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's terrible lying skills and desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I didn't do it! In fact, Chris, YOU did it!

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: What? No I didn't!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes you did, and I have witnesses. Checkmate.

jokes.text: Plus, Ben butt-dialed me last night. Okay, okay. And this is how Eleanor Roosevelt would kiss. Whoa. Eleanor likes the tongue.
jokes.setup: Ron revealing how he found out about the relationship
jokes.punchline: Overhearing their bizarre historical figure roleplay
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy. The specific historical roleplay is perfectly Leslie and mortifyingly specific.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I found out about you two when I overheard you in the office after hours.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh God, what did you hear?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You were having some kind of historical figure roleplay. You were Benjamin Franklin, Ben was Thomas Jefferson, and you were arguing about whether the Constitution should have a clause about waffles.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: In my defense, the Founding Fathers loved breakfast foods.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: It was educational!

jokes.text: Oh, President Reagan, my blazer popped open. Well, Maggie Thatcher, let me help you with that. It's gonna be a bumpy ride to this summit meeting. Our countries have had a very... special relationship...
jokes.setup: More of their overheard roleplay
jokes.punchline: Elaborate political leader seduction scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the roleplay concept. Perfectly captures Leslie's political obsession in romantic context.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Mr. Ambassador, I've been following your voting record on environmental protection...

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Well, I do care deeply about our planet's future.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Maybe we could discuss it over dinner? Somewhere private?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, is this a conflict of interest?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Only if you don't vote yes on my infrastructure bill.

jokes.text: In my heart, I'm sad you had to die. Li'I horse, spread your wings and learn to fly. Horses don't fly. That's why I'm telling him, 'learn to fly.'
jokes.setup: Andy's memorial song with impossible imagery
jokes.punchline: His logic that the impossibility is exactly why the horse needs to learn
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - completely backwards but delivered with total confidence.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: A horse with no name walked into a bar. The bartender said, 'Why the long face?' The horse said, 'Because I have no name.'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That doesn't make sense. Horses can't talk.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Exactly! That's why he needs to learn!

jokes.text: You're a champion little horse And you're dead You're the champion of death
jokes.setup: Attempting an uplifting memorial song
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making it entirely about death and being morbid
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his inability to read tone. The cheerful delivery of dark lyrics works.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We gather here today to celebrate a life well lived, so let me play you a little song I wrote.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Life is short and then you die, nothing matters in the end, we're all just worm food, my friends.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Dust to dust and bones to clay, we're all rotting every day, eventually we'll decompose and fade away.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So cherish the time that you have left before the darkness comes and takes your final breath.

jokes.text: Which one floats your penis? They're all black. Maybe to the layman, Jerry. Obsidian, onyx, midnight, lost soul, rolling blackout, sleeping panther, and void by Armani.
jokes.setup: Tom asking Jerry to choose between memorial ribbons
jokes.punchline: Elaborate designer names for what are just different blacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom Haverford materialism. The ridiculous color names and 'void by Armani' are excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Jerry, you gotta pick one. We need a ribbon color for the memorial.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, I'm thinking we go with the Midnight Obsidian.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: That's just black, Jerry.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: No, no. Or maybe the Ebony Noir?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Also black.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: What about Charcoal Shadow?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Jerry, they're all just different blacks!

jokes.text: Is tendonitis symptomatic of something larger? Really, that's the question you wanted to ask me? I'm just very worried.
jokes.setup: Chris building up to an important personal question
jokes.punchline: It's just more of his health anxiety, not anything relationship-related
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but predictable. Chris's self-absorption is well-established.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ann, I need to ask you something really important.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Oh my god, what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Do you think my heart rate is elevated? I've been feeling a flutter in my chest all morning.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris, you're fine.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: But what if it's arrhythmia? Or early-onset cardiomyopathy?

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: It's just more of his health anxiety, not anything relationship-related.

jokes.text: I won the MacArthur Genius Grant. Yeah. Yeah, so that's what the hug was all about.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben frantically covering for their romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Claiming Leslie won one of the most prestigious intellectual awards in America
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their terrible lying. The MacArthur Grant is absurdly prestigious for the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? No, we were just... Ben was helping me with my... my Fulbright application!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yes, that's right. Leslie won a Fulbright scholarship.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: One of the most prestigious intellectual awards in America.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We should probably tell people about this.

jokes.text: Coconut just took a dump on the stage. We gotta clean that up. Code red. Coconut just took a dump onstage.
jokes.setup: Solemn memorial service with live animals
jokes.punchline: Horse defecating during the ceremony
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Predictable but functional for the chaotic memorial setup.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: We've gathered here today to celebrate the life of—

dialogue.speaker: Horse
dialogue.line: *defecates loudly during ceremony*

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Well, I think that's what he would have wanted.

jokes.text: Hope you brought a change of clothes. 'Cause your eyes are about to piss tears. You nasty.
jokes.setup: Introducing the memorial video presentation
jokes.punchline: Crude and inappropriate metaphor for emotional impact
jokes.explanation: Very Jean-Ralphio - inappropriate tone and crude language for a memorial. Character-specific but not brilliant.

jokes.text: I'm Thelma, you're Louise. You can't die full of regret. Why don't you live your life like that cow from the video? He was a horse. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio making an inspirational point about Li'l Sebastian
jokes.punchline: He doesn't even know what kind of animal Li'l Sebastian was
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jean-Ralphio's superficial engagement with everything.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: He doesn't even know what kind of animal Li'l Sebastian was

jokes.text: What's 5,000 times better than a candle in the wind? This song is called 5,000 Candles in the Wind.
jokes.setup: Andy building anticipation for his memorial song
jokes.punchline: Literal multiplication of Elton John's famous song title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy logic - taking the request literally and mathematically. Perfect setup and payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I've been working on this song all week. It's gonna blow your minds. It's called 'Tiny Dancer Times Tiny Dancer.'

jokes.text: Up in horsey heaven Here's the thing You trade your legs for angel's wings And once we've all said goodbye You take a running leap and you learn to fly
jokes.setup: Memorial song attempting to be touching
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphors and the image of a horse taking a 'running leap' to fly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy songwriting - earnest but logically confused. The horse 'running leap' to flight is wonderfully absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: She was a bright light in a dark tunnel. A shining beacon of hope that soared like a majestic eagle... riding on the back of a dolphin.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And when life got her down, she would just take a running leap and fly away like a horse.

jokes.text: I think if you would know one thing about me, it would be that I prefer laying wreaths to lighting torches.
jokes.setup: Ron being moved to torch-lighting duty
jokes.punchline: Treating ceremonial preferences as a core personality trait
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan delivery. The idea that ceremonial preferences define someone is absurd but delivered seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't like it. Torches are for people who don't have the confidence to use a regular light switch.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, it's just for the opening ceremony.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: A torch is a symbol of weakness. I need to carry something that represents strength and independence. Like a flashlight. A very expensive flashlight.

jokes.text: I have cried twice in my life. Once when I was seven, and I was hit by a school bus. And then again when I heard that Li'I Sebastian had passed.
jokes.setup: Ron's emotional confession about crying
jokes.punchline: The horse's death affected him more than severe physical trauma
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Ron Swanson moment. Perfect escalation and character consistency. The juxtaposition is absurd but believable for Ron.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I cried once. When I was 7 and my horse died. I cried so hard that I was dehydrated for three days after that. Dated a woman for two years once. Sometime our love was passionate, sometimes it was affectionate. It was wonderful.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: She left me. I was not moved. And I cried when my horse died.

jokes.text: No, they were out. So I just got a big thing of lighter fluid. It's kind of the same thing. Huh? Oh! We planned that! Wow, wow!
jokes.setup: Jerry's substitution creating a dangerous situation
jokes.punchline: Massive explosion followed by Leslie's desperate cover-up
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup and Leslie's characteristic damage control. Jerry's incompetence pays off.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I substituted the nitrogen in the fertilizer with helium to make it lighter.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Jerry, that's not how chemistry works!

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Well, it seemed like a good idea at the time.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We need to evacuate immediately!

jokes.text: Ron, are you mad at me? 'Cause without eyebrows, I can't really tell.
jokes.setup: Ron's facial hair being burned off
jokes.punchline: Jerry's inability to read emotions without eyebrow cues
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy callback. Jerry's social awkwardness makes the line work.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I can't tell if Ron's angry or sad. His eyebrows are gone.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I'm both, Jerry.

jokes.text: You have no interest in government work, and you slow everything down with your selfish attitude. I'd love for you to stick around, Tommy. It'll be damn hard to replace you. But I'll support you either way.
jokes.setup: Ron listing Tom's negative qualities
jokes.punchline: Somehow concluding he wants Tom to stay despite these flaws
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - insulting someone while being supportive. Shows his complicated relationship with Tom.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tom is selfish, arrogant, and a terrible friend. He's also stupid, lazy, and has no real skills or talents whatsoever. He's a burden on this office and a burden on society.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: But... I need him. I don't know why, but I do. Please don't ever leave.

jokes.text: Two of my lovers in one place. What a coincidence.
jokes.setup: Tammy arriving at the memorial
jokes.punchline: Claiming both Ron and Glenn as lovers
jokes.explanation: Good Tammy line - predatory and uncomfortable. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Tammy II
dialogue.line: Ron! I heard about your little memorial service. How touching.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Tammy.

dialogue.speaker: Tammy II
dialogue.line: And Glenn! My two favorite men in one place. You both came to mourn me?

dialogue.speaker: Glenn Dusnick
dialogue.line: We're not—

dialogue.speaker: Tammy II
dialogue.line: Don't be modest. We all know we were lovers. Both of you. At the same time. It's fine, I'm not mad.

jokes.text: An hour ago, a giant fireball consumed my entire face. And it was far preferable to spending another second with you.
jokes.setup: Ron's burned face from the memorial disaster
jokes.punchline: Claiming severe burns are preferable to Tammy's company
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron line. Perfect escalation and character consistency. The hyperbole works because it's Ron vs. Tammy.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I would rather have severe burns on my face than spend one more second with Tammy.

jokes.text: I did do 10,000 push-ups last week. Oh, really. That might have something to do with it.
jokes.setup: Discussing causes of Chris's tendonitis
jokes.punchline: Absurdly excessive exercise routine revealed casually
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Chris's fitness extremism. The casual delivery sells the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris, you have to stop exercising so much. Your tendonitis is getting worse.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Ann, I only do a thousand push-ups every morning, a five-mile run before work, fifty pull-ups on my lunch break, and then I do CrossFit for three hours after work.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: That's insane!

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Plus on weekends I do Insanity workout videos and then I climb a mountain just for fun.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris, that's not normal exercise. That's a cry for help.

jokes.text: I'd much rather do 5,000 push-ups with a wonderful woman. Sitting on my back to increase my resistance.
jokes.setup: Chris making a romantic gesture
jokes.punchline: Turning it into a fitness optimization opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - turning romance into exercise. Character integration is excellent.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I've planned a romantic evening for us. I've calculated the optimal route through the park for a moonlit walk that maximizes calorie burn while minimizing joint impact.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Chris, that's not romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I've also prepared a low-sodium, high-protein dinner with a precise macronutrient ratio. And I color-coded the evening's schedule for maximum efficiency.

jokes.text: Eighteen bucks each, $40 for the set. That doesn't add up. You don't add up.
jokes.setup: Customer pointing out Andy's math error
jokes.punchline: Andy deflecting with a nonsensical personal attack
jokes.explanation: Good Andy response - completely illogical but delivered with confidence. The randomness fits his character.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: You charged me $12 for a $5 item.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well, you have a weird face.

jokes.text: Catching the number 12 bus to Satan's butthole? Actually, I prefer the number 69 train to Humpsville station.
jokes.setup: Donna making a crude joke about Glenn's destination
jokes.punchline: Glenn one-upping her with an even more explicit transportation innuendo
jokes.explanation: Sexual innuendo escalation. Glenn's unexpected participation works but relies on crude humor.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Where are you going, Glenn? To pound town?

dialogue.speaker: Glenn
dialogue.line: No, I'm taking the express train to deep tunnel station, population: me.

jokes.text: I saw her sweating real sexy on the StairMaster at Bally's. I need petty cash. My iPhone cover's gross and I need to get a new one.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining how he hired Tessa
jokes.punchline: Her immediate materialistic demand undermining any professional facade
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for both. Shows Jean-Ralphio's shallow hiring practices and Tessa's attitude.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: I hired Tessa because she's got real business acumen, you know? She understands the entrepreneurial spirit.

dialogue.speaker: Tessa
dialogue.line: Do I get a company car?

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: We haven't even started yet.

dialogue.speaker: Tessa
dialogue.line: A Range Rover?

jokes.text: Why do we keep our petty cash in a clear plastic toilet bowl? I don't know, maybe 'cause we're... Flush with cash
jokes.setup: Questioning the bizarre petty cash storage system
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun justifying the toilet bowl choice
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but fits Jean-Ralphio's terrible business decisions. The visual absurdity helps.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Why is the petty cash in the toilet bowl?

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Because it's a liquid asset.

jokes.text: We may not have any clients yet, but we're about to blow up. First you set up the pond, then you reel in the fish.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio defending their business strategy
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphors about building clientele with no actual plan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jean-Ralphio's delusional confidence and business illiteracy.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: You gotta strike while the iron is hot and plant your seeds in fertile ground, you know what I'm saying? That's how you build a client base.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: First you gotta get your foot in the door, then you climb the ladder, then you're gonna be rolling in it like a dog in mud.

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: It's all about the long game mixed with the short con, bro. You just gotta believe in yourself and also commit light fraud.

tmdb_overview: Leslie and the parks team put together a memorial service for a dearly departed friend. Meanwhile, Tom makes a big life decision, while Chris reacts badly to a health problem.
insight: Misdirection drives a character-heavy episode; 49 jokes land unevenly across 23 minutes.
